name: crossvit

members.name: CrossViTConfig
members.signature: CrossViTConfig(num_classes: int = 1000, in_channels: int = 3, image_size: int = 224, small_patch: int = 12, large_patch: int = 16, small_dim: int = 192, large_dim: int = 384, small_heads: int = 3, large_heads: int = 6, depth: int = 4, mlp_ratio: float = 4.0, dropout: float = 0.0)

methods.name: __init__
methods.signature: __init__(num_classes: int = 1000, in_channels: int = 3, image_size: int = 224, small_patch: int = 12, large_patch: int = 16, small_dim: int = 192, large_dim: int = 384, small_heads: int = 3, large_heads: int = 6, depth: int = 4, mlp_ratio: float = 4.0, dropout: float = 0.0)

members.summary: Configuration dataclass for every CrossViT variant.
members.extended:
`CrossViTConfig` is an immutable container that fully specifies
the architecture of a CrossViT (Chen et al., 2021).  CrossViT
couples two ViT branches that operate on *different patch sizes*
(`small_patch` and `large_patch`) and exchanges information
only through their CLS tokens via lightweight cross-attention.

parameters.name: num_classes
parameters.description:
Number of output classes for the classification head.
Defaults to `1000` (ImageNet-1k).

parameters.name: in_channels
parameters.description: Number of input image channels.  Defaults to `3` (RGB).

parameters.name: image_size
parameters.description:
Spatial side length of the square input image.  Both
`small_patch` and `large_patch` must divide `image_size`.
Defaults to `224`.

parameters.name: small_patch
parameters.description:
Patch size for the *small-patch* branch (fine-grained tokens).
Defaults to `12`.

parameters.name: large_patch
parameters.description:
Patch size for the *large-patch* branch (coarse tokens).
Defaults to `16`.

parameters.name: small_dim
parameters.description: Hidden width of the small-patch branch.  Defaults to `192`.

parameters.name: large_dim
parameters.description: Hidden width of the large-patch branch.  Defaults to `384`.

parameters.name: small_heads
parameters.description:
Number of attention heads in the small-patch branch.  Defaults
to `3`.

parameters.name: large_heads
parameters.description:
Number of attention heads in the large-patch branch.  Defaults
to `6`.

parameters.name: depth
parameters.description:
Number of CrossViT stages.  Each stage applies a self-attention
block on both branches followed by a bidirectional CLS-token
cross-attention exchange.  Defaults to `4`.

parameters.name: mlp_ratio
parameters.description: MLP expansion ratio inside both branches.  Defaults to `4.0`.

parameters.name: dropout
parameters.description:
Dropout probability after positional embeddings and inside
the MLP blocks.  Defaults to `0.0`.

examples:
Build a CrossViT-9 configuration with a 10-class head for CIFAR-10:
>>> from lucid.models.vision.crossvit import CrossViTConfig
>>> cfg = CrossViTConfig(
...     depth=3, small_dim=128, large_dim=256,
...     small_heads=4, large_heads=4, mlp_ratio=3.0,
...     num_classes=10,
... )
>>> cfg.small_dim, cfg.large_dim, cfg.depth, cfg.num_classes
(128, 256, 3, 10)
notes:
Reference: Chun-Fu (Richard) Chen *et al.*, *"CrossViT:
Cross-Attention Multi-Scale Vision Transformer for Image
Classification"*, ICCV 2021,
`[arXiv:2103.14899](https://arxiv.org/abs/2103.14899) <https://arxiv.org/abs/2103.14899>`_.

attributes.name: model_type
attributes.description: Constant string `"crossvit"` used by the model registry.

members.name: CrossViT

methods.name: forward
methods.summary: Update src CLS token using cross-attention over tgt tokens.

parameters.name: src
parameters.description: (B, N_src, dim_src) — source branch tokens (CLS at index 0)

parameters.name: tgt
parameters.description: (B, N_tgt, dim_tgt) — target branch tokens

members.extended:
CrossViT runs *two parallel ViT branches* with different patch
sizes (`small_patch` and `large_patch`), each maintaining its
own learnable CLS token and positional embedding.  Within each
CrossViT stage every branch first performs ordinary self-attention,
then the two branches exchange information *only through their CLS
tokens* using bidirectional cross-attention:

$$
\tilde{x}_{\text{cls}}^{\,l} = x_{\text{cls}}^{l} +
    \mathrm{Attn}\!\bigl(W_q x_{\text{cls}}^{l},\,
    W_k x_{\text{patch}}^{s},\, W_v x_{\text{patch}}^{s}\bigr),
$$
and symmetrically for the large-to-small direction.  Because
cross-attention only updates the CLS tokens, the multi-scale fusion
cost is $\mathcal{O}(N_s + N_l)$ instead of
$\mathcal{O}(N_s \cdot N_l)$.

`forward_features` returns the *large-branch* CLS token after
the final LayerNorm — a flat $(B, \texttt{large\_dim})$
feature.  For end-to-end classification use
`CrossViTForImageClassification`, which averages the logits
from both per-branch heads.

parameters.name: config
parameters.description:
Frozen dataclass specifying `image_size`, `small_patch`,
`large_patch`, `small_dim`, `large_dim`, `small_heads`,
`large_heads`, `depth`, `mlp_ratio`, `dropout`, and
`in_channels`.  See `CrossViTConfig`.

examples:
Build a CrossViT-9 backbone and run a forward pass:
>>> import lucid
>>> from lucid.models.vision.crossvit import CrossViT, CrossViTConfig
>>> cfg = CrossViTConfig(
...     depth=3, small_dim=128, large_dim=256,
...     small_heads=4, large_heads=4, mlp_ratio=3.0,
... )
>>> model = CrossViT(cfg)
>>> x = lucid.randn(1, 3, 224, 224)
>>> feat = model.forward_features(x)
>>> feat.shape                       # (B, large_dim)
(1, 256)
notes:
Reference: Chun-Fu Chen *et al.*, *"CrossViT: Cross-Attention
Multi-Scale Vision Transformer for Image Classification"*,
ICCV 2021, `[arXiv:2103.14899](https://arxiv.org/abs/2103.14899) <https://arxiv.org/abs/2103.14899>`_.

attributes.name: embed_s, embed_l
attributes.description: Patch-embedding convolutions for the small / large branches.

attributes.name: cls_s, cls_l
attributes.description: Learnable CLS tokens for the small / large branches.

attributes.name: pos_s, pos_l
attributes.description: Learnable positional embeddings for each branch.

attributes.name: pos_drop
attributes.description: Dropout applied right after the positional embedding sum.

attributes.name: norm_s, norm_l
attributes.description:
Final LayerNorms applied to each branch before extracting the
CLS token.

attributes.name: feature_info
attributes.description:
Single-stage feature description with channel count
`config.large_dim` and reduction `config.large_patch`.

members.name: CrossViTForImageClassification

methods.name: forward
methods.signature: forward(x: Tensor, labels: Tensor | None = None)
methods.summary: Update src CLS token using cross-attention over tgt tokens.

parameters.name: src
parameters.description: (B, N_src, dim_src) — source branch tokens (CLS at index 0)

parameters.name: tgt
parameters.description: (B, N_tgt, dim_tgt) — target branch tokens

members.summary: CrossViT with a dual-head classifier (Chen et al., 2021).
members.extended:
Wraps the same two-branch trunk as `CrossViT` and attaches
*one independent linear head per branch*.  Per paper §3.3, the
final prediction averages the two per-branch logits:

$$
\text{logits} = \tfrac{1}{2}\bigl(
    W_s\, z_{\text{cls}}^{s,(L)}
    + W_l\, z_{\text{cls}}^{l,(L)}
\bigr) + \tfrac{1}{2}(b_s + b_l),
$$
where $z_{\text{cls}}^{\cdot,(L)}$ denotes the post-LayerNorm
CLS token at the final stage of each branch.

Pass `labels` to `forward` to compute the cross-entropy
loss in the same pass.

parameters.name: config
parameters.description:
Architecture specification.  Must set `num_classes` to the
desired number of output categories.  See `CrossViTConfig`.

examples:
End-to-end inference with a CrossViT-9 classifier:
>>> import lucid
>>> from lucid.models.vision.crossvit import (
...     CrossViTConfig, CrossViTForImageClassification,
... )
>>> cfg = CrossViTConfig(
...     depth=3, small_dim=128, large_dim=256,
...     small_heads=4, large_heads=4, mlp_ratio=3.0,
... )
>>> model = CrossViTForImageClassification(cfg)
>>> x = lucid.randn(1, 3, 224, 224)
>>> out = model(x)
>>> out.logits.shape
(1, 1000)
notes:
Reference: Chen *et al.*, *"CrossViT: Cross-Attention Multi-Scale
Vision Transformer for Image Classification"*, ICCV 2021.

attributes.name: embed_s, embed_l
attributes.description: Patch-embedding convolutions for the small / large branches.

attributes.name: cls_s, cls_l
attributes.description: Learnable CLS tokens for each branch.

attributes.name: pos_s, pos_l
attributes.description: Learnable positional embeddings for each branch.

attributes.name: pos_drop
attributes.description: Dropout applied right after positional embedding.

attributes.name: classifier
attributes.description:
Small-branch classification head of shape
`(num_classes, small_dim)`.

attributes.name: head_l
attributes.description:
Large-branch classification head of shape
`(num_classes, large_dim)`, kept in sync with `classifier`
by `reset_classifier`.

members.name: crossvit_9
members.extended:
Builds the canonical *CrossViT-9* configuration: `depth=3`,
`small_dim=128`, `large_dim=256`, `small_patch=12`,
`large_patch=16`, `mlp_ratio=3.0`.  Approximately **8.6M
parameters** — the smallest variant in the paper.

parameters.name: pretrained
parameters.description:
If `True`, loads ImageNet-1k pretrained weights when
available in the model zoo.  Defaults to `False`.

parameters.name: **overrides
parameters.description:
Keyword overrides applied on top of the canonical CrossViT-9
config.  Each override must match a field of
`CrossViTConfig`.

returns.description:
A `CrossViT` backbone returning a flat $(B, 256)$
large-branch CLS feature.

examples:
>>> import lucid
>>> from lucid.models.vision.crossvit import crossvit_9
>>> model = crossvit_9()
>>> x = lucid.randn(1, 3, 224, 224)
>>> feat = model.forward_features(x)
>>> feat.shape
(1, 256)
notes:
CrossViT-9 reaches **73.9% top-1 on ImageNet-1k** (Chen et al.,
2021, Table 5).  See
`[arXiv:2103.14899](https://arxiv.org/abs/2103.14899) <https://arxiv.org/abs/2103.14899>`_.

members.name: crossvit_9_cls
members.extended:
Combines the `crossvit_9` backbone with two per-branch
linear heads whose logits are averaged (paper §3.3).  Default
output is `num_classes=1000` (ImageNet-1k).  ~8.6M parameters.

parameters.name: pretrained
parameters.description:
If `True`, loads ImageNet-1k pretrained weights when
available.  Defaults to `False`.

parameters.name: **overrides
parameters.description: Keyword overrides on top of the canonical CrossViT-9 config.

returns.description:
Classifier returning `ImageClassificationOutput` whose
`logits` has shape `(B, num_classes)`.

examples:
>>> import lucid
>>> from lucid.models.vision.crossvit import crossvit_9_cls
>>> model = crossvit_9_cls(num_classes=1000)
>>> x = lucid.randn(1, 3, 224, 224)
>>> model(x).logits.shape
(1, 1000)
notes:
CrossViT-9 reaches **73.9% top-1 on ImageNet-1k** (Chen et al.,
2021, Table 5).

members.name: crossvit_tiny
members.extended:
Tiny dual-branch ViT with a *single* CrossViT stage
(`depth=1`), `small_dim=96`, `large_dim=192`,
`mlp_ratio=4.0`.  Approximately **7.0M parameters** — useful for
rapid prototyping and mobile baselines.

parameters.name: pretrained
parameters.description:
If `True`, loads ImageNet-1k pretrained weights when
available.  Defaults to `False`.

parameters.name: **overrides
parameters.description: Keyword overrides on top of the canonical CrossViT-Tiny config.

returns.description:
A `CrossViT` backbone returning a flat $(B, 192)$
large-branch CLS feature.

examples:
>>> import lucid
>>> from lucid.models.vision.crossvit import crossvit_tiny
>>> model = crossvit_tiny()
>>> x = lucid.randn(1, 3, 224, 224)
>>> model.forward_features(x).shape
(1, 192)

members.name: crossvit_tiny_cls
members.extended:
Combines the `crossvit_tiny` backbone (`depth=1`,
`small_dim=96`, `large_dim=192`) with two per-branch linear
heads whose logits are averaged.  ~7.0M parameters — useful as a
fast baseline for mobile / latency-sensitive applications.

parameters.name: pretrained
parameters.description:
If `True`, loads ImageNet-1k pretrained weights when
available.  Defaults to `False`.

parameters.name: **overrides
parameters.description: Keyword overrides on top of the canonical CrossViT-Tiny config.

examples:
>>> import lucid
>>> from lucid.models.vision.crossvit import crossvit_tiny_cls
>>> model = crossvit_tiny_cls(num_classes=1000)
>>> x = lucid.randn(1, 3, 224, 224)
>>> model(x).logits.shape
(1, 1000)

members.name: crossvit_small
members.extended:
Wider / deeper CrossViT with `depth=4`, `small_dim=192`,
`large_dim=384`, `mlp_ratio=4.0`.  Approximately **26.9M
parameters** — the standard small-class variant in the paper.

parameters.name: pretrained
parameters.description:
If `True`, loads ImageNet-1k pretrained weights when
available.  Defaults to `False`.

parameters.name: **overrides
parameters.description: Keyword overrides on top of the canonical CrossViT-Small config.

returns.description:
A `CrossViT` backbone returning a flat $(B, 384)$
large-branch CLS feature.

examples:
>>> import lucid
>>> from lucid.models.vision.crossvit import crossvit_small
>>> model = crossvit_small()
>>> x = lucid.randn(1, 3, 224, 224)
>>> model.forward_features(x).shape
(1, 384)

members.name: crossvit_small_cls
members.extended:
Combines the `crossvit_small` backbone (`depth=4`,
`small_dim=192`, `large_dim=384`) with two per-branch linear
heads whose logits are averaged.  ~26.9M parameters.

parameters.name: pretrained
parameters.description:
If `True`, loads ImageNet-1k pretrained weights when
available.  Defaults to `False`.

parameters.name: **overrides
parameters.description: Keyword overrides on top of the canonical CrossViT-Small config.

examples:

members.name: crossvit_base
members.extended:
Deeper variant with `depth=10` CrossViT stages,
`small_dim=192`, `large_dim=384`, `mlp_ratio=4.0`.
Approximately **105M parameters** — trades parameter count for
accuracy by lengthening the trunk rather than widening it.

parameters.name: pretrained
parameters.description:
If `True`, loads ImageNet-1k pretrained weights when
available.  Defaults to `False`.

parameters.name: **overrides
parameters.description: Keyword overrides on top of the canonical CrossViT-Base config.

returns.description:
A `CrossViT` backbone returning a flat $(B, 384)$
large-branch CLS feature.

examples:
>>> import lucid
>>> from lucid.models.vision.crossvit import crossvit_base
>>> model = crossvit_base()
>>> x = lucid.randn(1, 3, 224, 224)
>>> model.forward_features(x).shape
(1, 384)

members.extended:
Combines the `crossvit_base` backbone (`depth=10`,
`small_dim=192`, `large_dim=384`) with two per-branch linear
heads whose logits are averaged.  ~105M parameters.

members.name: crossvit_18
members.extended:
Builds the *CrossViT-18* configuration: `depth=3`,
`small_dim=224`, `large_dim=448`, `small_heads=7`,
`large_heads=7`, `mlp_ratio=3.0`.  Approximately **43.3M
parameters** — the largest variant in the paper.

summary: CrossViT family — backbone + image classification (Chen et al., 2021).
citation: Chen, Chun-Fu (Richard), et al. "CrossViT: Cross-Attention Multi-Scale Vision Transformer for Image Classification." Proceedings of the IEEE/CVF International Conference on Computer Vision, 2021, pp. 357-366.
theory:
CrossViT explicitly models *multi-scale* visual information by
running two parallel ViT branches with different patch sizes and
fusing them through a lightweight cross-attention mechanism.  A
*small-patch* branch (e.g. patch size 12) processes a long sequence
of fine-grained tokens, while a *large-patch* branch (e.g. patch
size 16) processes a short sequence of coarse tokens.  Each branch
has its own class token $x_{\text{cls}}^{s}$ /
$x_{\text{cls}}^{l}$ and is independently transformer-encoded
for $L$ blocks.

Information is exchanged between branches only through the class
tokens, using *cross-attention*: the class token of one branch
attends to the patch tokens of the *other* branch as queries,
leaving the other branch's patch tokens unchanged.  Concretely, for
the small-to-large direction,



$$
\tilde{x}_{\text{cls}}^{\,l} = x_{\text{cls}}^{l} +
    \mathrm{Attn}\!\bigl(W_q x_{\text{cls}}^{l},
    W_k x_{\text{patch}}^{s}, W_v x_{\text{patch}}^{s}\bigr),
$$

and symmetrically for large-to-small.  Because cross-attention is
only $\mathcal{O}(N_s + N_l)$ per direction, multi-scale
fusion is essentially free relative to the per-branch self-attention
cost.  The final classifier averages the two updated class tokens.
The variants CrossViT-9 / 15 / 18 trade depth and width along this
two-branch skeleton.
